preview_text:
The model rates England as slight favorite with a 49% win probability, while Croatia's chance to prevail stands at 21%. This represents a meaningful gap, though England's advantage is not commanding—the model does not project overwhelming dominance from the higher-ranked side.

The draw at 30% suggests competitive balance despite England's edge. A one-goal margin or stalemate are plausible outcomes; the spread across the three results is fairly broad, indicating genuine uncertainty about how the match will unfold rather than a heavily skewed contest.

The sportsbook consensus leans more heavily toward an England victory, assigning them 56%, a gap of 7 percentage points above the model. The market also compresses the draw probability to 25% while Croatia drifts to 19%. This divergence suggests the books are pricing England's advantage more steeply than the statistical forecast. The dashboard makes no claim as to which view is more accurate. Polymarket has not yet posted a per-match contract for this fixture.
